@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried the correct support.Case and casehistory__c tables and implemented the right logic, but in the execute_python step it only stripped leading '#' and failed to trim trailing whitespace from agent IDs before merging transfer counts with handled agents, causing misaligned joins and an incorrect selection (returned 005Wt000003NBcAIAW instead of the ground-truth 005Wt000003NIliIAG).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the owner with the fewest created cases by querying "Case" (call_ZucAgs927kawljwYkARjvKFE) and counting ownerid occurrences in execute_python, rather than counting transfer events from casehistory within the last 4 quarters, thus answering a different metric than requested.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After querying casehistory__c and getting empty results for owner transfer events, the agent prematurely concluded that no agent qualified instead of selecting the agent with the minimum (zero) transfer count among those with >0 cases (which it had retrieved from "Case"), thereby stopping early and not completing the required computation.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried support.casehistory__c using an incorrect filter/value combination (SELECT newvalue__c ... WHERE field__c = 'Owner' for 2022-04-10 to 2023-04-10), which returned an empty result and led it to conclude there were no transfers instead of correctly filtering (e.g., field__c = 'OwnerId') and counting by the prior owner, so it failed to return the correct agent Id.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes

After querying casehistory__c for OwnerId changes grouped by newvalue__c (receiving agent) and getting an empty result, the agent prematurely concluded there were no transfers and returned no Id, instead of attributing transfers to the old owner and identifying the minimum among agents who handled >0 cases (as evidenced by the final contributing queries call_DxPbWMOMMq8YpUuABnZt7idB and call_qhrgb7zpoZ10DdwBGLO1rXgm).
